title: Europe takes the stage at the Festival d'Avignon
content:
Since its creation in 1947, the festival has built on independent artistic excellence and creative freedom by bringing together productions and artists from across Europe and beyond. As such, it also offers a unique glimpse into the European collaboration that help performing arts flourishing across borders.

Behind some of the productions presented on stage and the professional encounters throughout the city, the European Union’s Creative Europe programme is helping artists create, connect and reach new audiences.

Among this year's programme are CAPRA (une chèvre), directed by Jeanne Candel, and La Parabole du Seum by Rébecca Chaillon. Both received support through Prospero – New European Wave, a platform co-funded by Creative Europe that connects emerging artists with theatres to develop and present new works through international collaboration.

The festival is also expanding its European partnerships. It has recently joined the Creative Europe cooperation project “EUphoric Youth”, which brings together 11 cultural organisations to encourage young people from across Europe to actively participate in artistic, cultural and democratic life.

title: Culture Moves Europe: 2,542 grantees selected
content:
Between October 2025 and April 2026, for the call for individual mobility we received a record number of 7,831 applications, requesting €27,155,425 in total.

After careful consideration from the jury and evaluators, 1,596 projects were awarded a Culture Moves Europe (CME) grant. 2,542 artists and cultural professionals, who requested €5,499,825 in total, were directly supported.

Culture Moves Europe serves as an entry point for EU funding: 76% of applicants had no previous experience applying to other EU funding schemes.

The average grantee receives around 20% more financial support than they did in the 2022-2025 edition of CME.

We also see an increase in people benefiting from inclusion top-ups and accessibility support in this edition of the individual mobility call.

  • 12% of grantees claimed the family top-up – around double the number in CME 2022-2025
  • 3% of grantees claimed the Overseas Countries and Territories/ Outermost Regions (OCT/OR) top-up
  • Out of the 46 projects involving OCT/OR travel, 46% involved people residing in one these territories
  • 3% of grantees claimed the visa top-up
  • Overall, nearly 1 in 5 of grantees expect to benefit from an inclusion top-up or accessibility support
snippet: Between October 2025 and April 2026, for the call for individual mobility we received a record number of 7,831 applications, requesting €27,155,425 in total.
creator: csudaga

title: Open Call of Perform Europe 2026 – 2028
content:
After two highly successful editions (2020-2022 / 2023-2026), Perform Europe will continue to put emphasis on exploring, with the performing arts sector, how cross-border touring can be made fairer, greener, and more inclusive.

Proposals are submitted by collaboration partnerships of performing arts professionals and organisations. Partners should work on their proposal together. This is a call to the sector to create partnerships and propose impactful ideas that can generate real change via two main priorities:

  • Inclusion and diversity
  • Fight against climate change

    • Each proposal should choose one of the above main priorities to focus on, while still taking into account and implementing measures related to the second priority.

    In addition, all proposals should aim for quality and innovation, be built on fair practices, and are encouraged to promote collaboration across different regions within the 41 Creative Europe countries.

    The open call was launched on 23 June 2026. We invite you now to build partnerships, jointly develop proposals, and submit them through our website before 22 October 2026, 23:59 CEST.

    Perform Europe will grant €1.4 million to at least 25 partnerships.
snippet: After two highly successful editions (2020-2022 / 2023-2026), Perform Europe will continue to put emphasis on exploring, with the performing arts sector, how cross-border touring can be made fairer,
creator: csudaga

title: Eight European Heritage Days Stories awarded grants in 2026
content:
Eight inspiring community-led initiatives from across Europe have been selected to receive funding through the 2026 European Heritage Days Stories grant scheme.

Drawing inspiration from stories shared through the European Heritage Days programme, the projects will help communities safeguard, reinterpret and promote heritage under the 2026 theme, heritage at risk: revive, resist, reimagine.

  • The Last Voices (Albania)
  • Résister, Réinventer, Raviver (Belgium)
  • From Votive Offering to Heritage: The Saints’ Guard Between Past and Present (Croatia)
  • Miettilä Ghost-Map (Finland)
  • Reclaiming the Historic Avas – Community Renewal of Neglected Heritage (Hungary)
  • Architects of Peace: Women and Political Power in Medieval European Borderlands (11th–13th centuries) (Spain)
  • Science History at Risk: Revive, Resist and Reimagine the Heritage of Knowledge from Bergara (Spain)
  • Breaking Heritage Ambassadors Project (United Kingdom)

    • Together, these projects demonstrate that safeguarding heritage is not only about protecting monuments and historic sites. It is also about preserving memories, knowledge, traditions and cultural practices that help communities understand their past and imagine their future.
snippet: Eight inspiring community-led initiatives from across Europe have been selected to receive funding through the 2026 European Heritage Days Stories grant scheme.
creator: csudaga

title: New EU report: the multiple roles of public libraries should be recognised and reinforced
content:
There are an estimated 67 000 public libraries serving 757 million visitors and 75 million registered users each year in Europe.

These libraries represent a significant, yet underused, asset for Europe and Europeans in addressing social, cultural and educational challenges.

To examine how to strengthen and promote the multiple roles of public libraries, 36 experts from 25 EU countries came together in an Open Method of Coordination (OMC) Group mandated by the EU council.

The group has now published its findings. The purpose of its report is to trigger debates and actions within and outside the librarian community.

Investing in public libraries benefits society and democracy in the long run, study finds.

Link to the Publications Office of the EU.

This report is an inspiration for policymakers at local, national, and European levels, encouraging them to share knowledge to build knowledge.

The European Commission remains committed to enhancing public libraries as vibrant, inclusive forums, promoting access to information, quality of life, and shared values.
snippet: There are an estimated 67 000 public libraries serving 757 million visitors and 75 million registered users each year in Europe.
creator: csudaga

title: Factsheet: A joint commitment for culture in the European Union
content: For the first time in EU history, the European Parliament, the Council of the European Union and the European Commission have signed a joint declaration setting out their shared political commitment to protecting, promoting and supporting culture in Europe. Find out more in this factsheet.
snippet: For the first time in EU history, the European Parliament, the Council of the European Union and the European Commission have signed a joint declaration setting out their shared political commitment
